team_news.notes: Switzerland have a clean bill of health and are near full-strength, with Yakin only considering minor attacking rotation (Manzambi potentially in for Vargas/Okafor). Bosnia-Herzegovina are hit by multiple injury concerns: Nidal Celik is ruled out of the entire tournament (replaced by Arjan Malic), captain Edin Dzeko missed the opener with a shoulder injury and remains doubtful, Haris Tabakovic is also ruled out, and Sead Kolasinac faces a late fitness test after limping off against Canada.

team_news.notes: Canada captain Alphonso Davies (hamstring) is now declared available but likely to be managed off the bench or used sparingly, while defender Moise Bombito (tibia/leg) is expected to miss out again; striker Cyle Larin is set to replace Tani Oluwaseyi in a rotation hint from Marsch. Qatar came through their opener against Switzerland unscathed with no injuries or suspensions, and Lopetegui is expected to name an unchanged XI, though Almoez Ali is pushing to start over Yusuf Abdurisag up front.

team_news.notes: Haiti report a fully fit squad with no injury concerns ahead of their first World Cup match in 52 years. Scotland are without key midfielder Billy Gilmour (knee injury, tournament-ending), while Scott McTominay (illness) and Scott McKenna (knock) were late doubts but both are expected to start; Tommy Conway also misses out with an ankle problem.

team_news.notes: Belgium's Zeno Debast is ruled out with injury and Romelu Lukaku starts on the bench after a season hampered by a long-term hamstring problem, with Charles De Ketelaere leading the attack; Egypt's Mohamed Salah recovered from an April hamstring injury in time to start, but fitness concerns lingered heading into the match.

team_news.notes: Iran are without Alireza Jahanbakhsh (muscle), Dennis Eckert, and Mehdi Torabi (both unspecified), with Roozbeh Cheshmi also a late fitness doubt, and Sardar Azmoun left out for disciplinary reasons — Taremi leads a depleted but functional attack. New Zealand lose Matt Garbett to a confirmed pre-tournament hamstring injury (replaced by Logan Rogerson), Ryan Thomas is also out for the opener, and captain Chris Wood carries fitness concerns after a knee injury kept him out for six months.

team_news.notes: Spain start without injured Fermín López (fractured metatarsal, tournament miss) and begin Lamine Yamal & Nico Williams on the bench after hamstring recoveries, with Víctor Muñoz also a late doubt (muscular overload); De la Fuente opted to rotate the wingers in as impact subs. Cape Verde have no reported injuries or suspensions and field a full squad.

team_news.notes: Saudi Arabia are near full strength with only Nawaf Al-Aqidi (muscle) confirmed out and coach Donis reporting a largely healthy squad; Salem Al-Dawsari leads the line as captain. team_news.notes: France are near full strength — Saliba, Koundé, and Tchouameni all cleared minor injury concerns, with only Hugo Ekitike confirmed absent long-term; Deschamps expected to field his strongest XI with no rotation hints. team_news.notes: Argentina were near full-strength despite pre-tournament concerns: Messi (mild hamstring), Álvarez (ankle) and Martínez (fractured finger) all cleared to play, with Tagliafico monitored but available; Otamendi served a one-match suspension (red card vs Ecuador in qualifying). Algeria's sole confirmed absence was LB Ramy Bensebaini (ankle injury, ruled out), leaving Petkovic without a key left-back option.

team_news.notes: Portugal had no confirmed injuries or suspensions, with Roberto Martinez fielding a fully fit squad; rotation saw Leao and Conceicao benched in favour of Pedro Neto and Bernardo Silva. team_news.notes: Uzbekistan reported no injury concerns heading into the match, fielding a full-strength squad led by captain Eldor Shomurodov and Abbosbek Fayzullaev under Fabio Cannavaro's 3-4-2-1 system.
